title: Skill delivery enforced via hooks (verification pipeline), not hoped for via skill files
context: Zod bench 2026-07-08: fable-mode skill installed as a file was read in 0/20 S-arm sessions across Haiku/Sonnet/Opus — pass rates identical to bare arms (21/21 pair agreement). Forcing the read (prompt naming the skill) flipped FAIL→PASS on the discriminating task zod-5937 for both Opus and Haiku; rewriting the skill description as a pushy trigger got Opus to read it organically and pass, but Haiku still ignored it. Every PASS had a transcript-verified Skill invocation; every FAIL had none.
decision: Ship delivery as harness enforcement: src/core/pipeline.ts + hunch hook handles PostToolUse (records product edits + verify-shaped commands as observable facts) and Stop (refuses turn-end with unverified product edits, max 2 blocks/turn). Operating loop injected at SessionStart. No new config knob — firmness governs (advisory=inject+nag, firm/strict=stop-gate); HUNCH_PIPELINE=0 kills. State is per-session tmpdir scratch, never .hunch/. Released as v1.4.0.
consequences: Turn-end at firm/strict requires a verify-shaped command after the last product edit or an explicit impossibility statement
Gate evaluates observable facts only (commands, exit-shaped patterns), never model claims
Max-2-blocks per turn guarantees a broken gate degrades to advisory, never a lockout
Docs, .claude/ and .hunch/ edits never arm the gate
alternatives_rejected: Pushy skill descriptions alone — works for Opus-class, measured insufficient for Haiku-class (ignored even MANDATORY phrasing)
Naming the skill in every prompt — works but depends on the user remembering; not a product mechanism
New pipeline config knob — firmness already expresses enforcement appetite; a second knob invites contradictory states
Storing pipeline state in .hunch/ — it is derived, disposable, single-writer scratch; .hunch is the durable graph
